query_normalized: exploring the impact of data imputation methods on causal inference statistics
rationale: The paper focuses on the theoretical feasibility of inferring causal structures from uncontrolled data using algebraic geometry, but it does not address the specific mechanism of "data imputation" or evaluate how missing data handling methods impact causal inference statistics. It fails to meet criteria (a) or (b) as it studies a different methodological domain (algebraic constraints vs. imputation algorithms) and does not provide an empirical baseline or canonical reference for the specific pro
